sourceMovie.title: Bedazzled
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Bedazzled for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Harold Ramis keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Enchanted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bedazzled clicked for you, Enchanted (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bedazzled?
faq.answer: Try Enchanted, Penelope, Mannequin—each shares DNA with Bedazzled while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bedazzled?
faq.answer: Bedazzled (2000) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bedazzled worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
